conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting.
org_constraint: Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding jargon and ensuring clarity.
new_constraint: The phrase "Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out!" must appear at least once in the email.
instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting. Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding jargon and ensuring clarity. The phrase "Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out!" must appear at least once in the email.
response:
Subject: Resignation - [Your Name]

Dear [Boss's Name],

Please accept this email as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Your Job Title] at [Company Name]. My last day of employment will be [Your Last Day - usually two weeks from the date of the email].

This was not an easy decision, but I have decided to pursue a new opportunity that aligns with my long-term career goals.

I want to express my sincere gratitude for the opportunities I have been given during my time at [Company Name]. I have genuinely valued my experience here and appreciate the support and guidance you have provided me.

I am committed to ensuring a smooth transition during my remaining weeks. Please let me know how I can best assist in wrapping up my current projects and training my replacement.

To be completely honest, sometimes I feel like just shouting it out there: Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out! But on a more serious note, I am dedicated to making these next [Number] weeks as productive as possible for the team.

Thank you again for everything. I wish you and [Company Name] all the best for the future.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Phone Number - Optional]<turn|>
